text: Scientific Text Knowledge Extraction is still a hard task for machine learning algorithms. We present a very simple algorithm that at the same time does named entity recognition and relation detection, and evaluate the use of pre-trained language models (GPT-3, Llama-2) that are fine-tuned for the knowledge extraction task to derive relevant records of the derived knowledge (entities, and relations) related to complex science such as dopants in host materials, metal-organic frameworks and common composition/phase/morphology/application information. These are derived from sentences or from blocks of text (paragraphs), and output may be in the form of plain sentences, or in structured formats like a list of JSON objects. Hence this approach provides an extremely simple way to get large data bases of specialized scientific knowledge as derived from articles of research.
